creator: raimeetravel
caption:
Keeping it real about my finances & travel…

I think it can be very easy to assume without knowing the full picture, so these are just a few of the ways I’ve made travel possible for me over the last 10 years. 

Do I wish I had more money saved? Sometimes. All the time. 
Do I regret any of the trips I’ve done? Nope. 

Another thing I didn’t mention in the video that has helped me is always having multiple income streams — 

No matter what job I’ve had, I’ve ALWAYS picked up side projects & random gigs — Ive been a valet, a bartender, a background extra, a content creator!! the list goes on…

I completely understand if travel feels (and IS) out of reach, especially with the way things are right now. 

But I’ll always try to help show you all of the options, to help you do it for less than you thought!! 

🫶🏻✈️
.
.
@imperfecttaylor inspired me to post this because of her “living below my means” series! Go to her page and watch it!! 💓 

raimee, Raimee travel, Raimee Iacofano

transcription.text: This is how I'm still affording travel even in this economy for the past five years or so I've kept my rent payments really low. I currently live in a not-so-great apartment. Like what what is this? But I also haven't had a car since 2020. So no car payment. No car insurance payment. I very rarely buy new clothes I actually rent my clothes and when I do buy clothes, it's very high quality Classic pieces that will last forever. I get my nails done maybe once or twice a year I do splurge on my hair though, and I'm annoyingly strategic about my credit card points and miles I plan when I get a new card around a time when I know I'm going to have some big expenses that way I can get A great signup bonus and use that to fly for essentially free all of those things help me put thousands towards travel each year I want to keep it real and tell you guys too that I have also not been able to save that much money I've never had a 401k. I do invest myself each month in a few different accounts, but I can't touch that money now I've known that this lifestyle won't last forever. This is just what I've been prioritizing in my 20s and early 30s So right now this is how I make travel work in my life I'd rather see the world while I'm young and able even if I have to sacrifice some things who knows maybe one day I'll get a house and settle down, but I don't think I'll ever be able to afford that no matter what I do
